headline: AI model releases hit overdrive — Claude Opus 4.6 rules SWE-bench, OpenAI's GPT-5.4 drops w/ million-token context, and Anthropic just killed long-context pricing surcharges

stories.title: Claude Opus 4.6 Officially Dominates Coding Benchmarks
stories.summary: Anthropic's flagship 4.6 Opus hits 75.6% on SWE-bench and ships a 1M token context window in beta—basically a codebase's entire life story in one prompt. Sonnet 4.6 does near-Opus work at Sonnet prices, lowkey making it the budget pick devs actually prefer 70% of the time.
stories.takeaway: fr, if you're doing serious software engineering rn, Opus 4.6 is your move. Costs haven't changed but you're getting wild context + better reasoning out of box.

stories.title: OpenAI Retires GPT-5.1 Models, Bumps ChatGPT to 5.3/5.4
stories.summary: As of March 11, GPT-5.1 is straight up gone from ChatGPT—all convos auto-migrate to 5.3/5.4 variants. Plus new interactive math modules let you play w/ equations in real time across 70+ topics.
stories.takeaway: If you're still running 5.1 anywhere, migration is automatic, but check your API usage bc new models hit different pricing tiers.

stories.title: Anthropic Drops Pricing Surcharge for Million-Token Context
stories.summary: No more 100% premium for long-context requests—Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 now offer 1M tokens at standard pricing. Media limit jumped from 100 to 600 images per request too, making bulk doc processing way cheaper.
stories.takeaway: tbh this is huge if you work w/ long docs. Suddenly context windows aren't a luxury tax anymore—you're just paying normal rates regardless if prompt is 9k or 900k tokens.

stories.title: Pentagon/Anthropic Dispute Highlights AI Governance Gaps
stories.summary: Pentagon labeled Anthropic a supply-chain risk after refusing to enable mass surveillance + autonomous weapons use. 30+ OpenAI & Google DeepMind employees publicly sided w/ Anthropic in court filings, calling the move arbitrary.
stories.takeaway: This is lowkey the biggest AI governance story rn—shows major companies are willing to push back on military applications. Sets precedent for what 'responsible AI' actually means in practice.

watchNext.title: Meta's Avocado Model Delayed to May/June After Performance Issues
watchNext.why: Meta's big AI pushback got stalled—their next-gen LLM fell between Google Gemini 2.5 & 3 in benchmarks. Worth watching bc it signals whether they can catch up to frontier labs or if the gap's growing wider.

watchNext.title: Google DeepMind Launches AI Co-Scientist for DOE National Labs
watchNext.why: First real use case of agentic AI for hard science—Gemini-powered system already proposing drug candidates & predicting antimicrobial resistance. Shows where frontier AI actually creates value beyond benchmarks.
